basics.name: Lianghui Yi
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: Software Engineer with 5+ years building production systems at scale, specializing in full-stack development, cloud infrastructure, and AI-driven automation. Proven expertise shipping end-to-end solutions for enterprise customers, from frontend interfaces to distributed backend systems. Deep experience with Python, TypeScript, AWS, and agentic AI systems that power 50,000+ users.

work.name: 24 Helpful, LLC
highlights: Designed and shipped a self-evolving code system with generator/evaluator loops that accelerated development velocity; implemented CI/CD automation with GitHub Actions to close iteration cycles in under 24 hours.

Delivered end-to-end multi-tenant AWS cloud infrastructure for enterprise customers, reducing provisioning costs by 50% through automated resource management, tenant isolation, and infrastructure-as-code practices.

Collaborated directly with customers to scope technical requirements, remove deployment blockers, and accelerate production rollouts; codified deployment playbooks and runbooks to standardize operational response across systems.

work.name: 24Haowan Network Technology Co., Ltd.
highlights: Designed and shipped a full-stack SaaS product that achieved $1,000,000 ARR in 2 years through data-driven iteration, optimized user onboarding, and continuous product lifecycle refinement.
Led cross-functional engineering team; built internal tools and infrastructure that streamlined product and engineering workflows, improving team delivery cadence and reducing deployment friction.
Engaged directly with enterprise customers to scope technical requirements and uncover pain points, converting 3 strategic accounts within 3 months and validating product-market fit.

work.name: Maiduote Network Technology Co., Ltd.
highlights: Led end-to-end full-stack product development and launched a production platform that increased user engagement by 30%; integrated predefined forms and chatbots in collaboration with cross-functional stakeholders.
Scoped and delivered data-driven features across the full stack, coordinating cross-functional teams to prioritize roadmap items and ensure stable, rapid rollouts to production.
Drove customer-facing growth strategies that acquired over 50,000 users in 6 months, exceeding targets through targeted outreach and workflow optimization.

projects.name: tokenman.io
projects.description: Claude Code Hosting & AI Repo Self-Evolution Platform
highlights: Built terminal.tokenman.io — a multi-tenant Claude Code CLI hosting SaaS on EC2 with mobile-first SSH, persistent tmux sessions, and cloud-init provisioning for secure production access.

Designed a skill-based repo self-evolution system with shared AI context and slash commands, enabling autonomous AI-driven code loops via GitHub Actions with human + AI and headless generator/evaluator workflows.

projects.name: aInbox (ainbox.io)
highlights: Built an MCP-native AI agent middleware platform giving LLMs a dedicated inbox and async messaging layer, operated entirely via Model Context Protocol tools with no traditional UI.

Designed full server architecture with OAuth, JWT, and Streamable HTTP transport; implemented LLM-driven triage and labeling pipeline deployed on multi-tenant AWS infrastructure using Aurora Serverless, SES, and per-user IAM roles.

projects.name: AgentFolio
highlights: Built an agentic portfolio engine that detects visitor context and dynamically adapts resume content using LLM-powered rewriting and JD-matching pipelines; deployed on pure GitHub infrastructure with zero third-party cost.

Designed a five-stage agent architecture (perceive → reason → act → learn → explain) with RAG-powered chat, automated JD fetching, and visitor feedback loops that continuously optimize content resonance.

skills.name: Cloud & Infrastructure
keywords: AWS (EC2, SES, Aurora Serverless, IAM, SCPs)

education.institution: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications

volunteer.summary: Led community initiatives and organized weekly founder-focused events (1,000+ participants in one year), coordinated volunteers and facilitated connections to strengthen engagement and collaboration.
